title: Southernside West Affordable Community Set to Break Ground in Greenville, South Carolina
author: Unknown
description: HHAD and the Greenville Housing Fund announced the development of Southernside West, a 54-unit affordable senior housing community in Greenville, S.C. The four-story development will serve residents aged 55+ earning 30-80% of area median income, with completion expected in October 2027. The project is financed through partnerships with CommunityWorks, Huntington Bank, and Freddie Mac.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Huntington Bank is providing construction loans and tax credit equity for the project, demonstrating active participation in community development financing and affordable housing initiatives.

title: Huntington Bancshares Incorporated Declares Quarterly Cash Dividends On Its Common and Preferred Stocks
author: Prnewswire
description: Huntington Bancshares announced quarterly cash dividends on its common stock at $0.155 per share, unchanged from the prior quarter, payable July 1, 2026. The board also declared dividends on six series of preferred stocks with varying rates ranging from 4.45% to 6.875%, payable in July and August 2026.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The company maintained its common stock dividend at $0.155 per share with no increase from the prior quarter, indicating stable but not growing shareholder returns. While dividend declarations are generally positive for income investors, the unchanged dividend rate suggests no acceleration in capital returns or earnings growth momentum.

title: Huntington Bancshares Incorporated Declares Cash Dividend On Its Series I & Series L Preferred Stock
author: Prnewswire
description: Huntington Bancshares Incorporated announced quarterly cash dividends on its preferred stock series. The Board declared a dividend of $356.25 per share on the 5.70% Series I Preferred Stock, payable June 1, 2026, and $343.75 per share on the 5.50% Series L Preferred Stock, payable May 20, 2026.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The company's declaration of regular quarterly cash dividends on preferred stock demonstrates financial stability and commitment to returning capital to shareholders. Consistent dividend payments are typically viewed as a positive indicator of company health and confidence in future cash flows.

title: Nasdaq Texas Launches with Inaugural Dual Listings
author: Nasdaq, Inc.
description: Nasdaq Texas officially launched as a dual listing exchange on March 5, 2026, with SEC approval of its listing rules. The exchange, domiciled in Texas, allows U.S. public companies to leverage Texas' business-friendly environment while maintaining access to Nasdaq's global platform and technology. Five companies completed the inaugural dual listing cohort, and Nasdaq Inc. itself dual listed on the new exchange to demonstrate confidence in the platform.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Nasdaq is expanding its market presence by launching Nasdaq Texas and dual listing itself on the new exchange, demonstrating confidence in the platform and commitment to Texas' economic growth.
APA is part of the inaugural dual listing cohort and expressed pride in its Texas roots and commitment to shareholder value creation through participation in this strategic initiative.
Construction Partners is a charter member of Nasdaq Texas' first cohort, positioning itself to benefit from the new exchange's business-friendly environment and Nasdaq's technology.
J.B. Hunt is among the first dual-listed companies and emphasized its deep regional roots and commitment to supporting Texas' supply chain and economic growth.
Huntington is the first bank to dual list on Nasdaq Texas, aligning with the state's growth and innovation focus while gaining access to the new exchange's capabilities.

title: Stock Market Today, Jan. 22: Huntington Bancshares Falls After Missing Revenue and EPS Estimates
author: Josh Kohn-Lindquist
description: Huntington Bancshares stock fell 6.02% on January 22, 2026, after missing Q4 2025 revenue and EPS estimates due to higher expenses and credit provisions. Despite 12% revenue growth, the bank's criticized asset ratio rose and management guided for 10% expense increases in 2026. However, management expects strong growth in net interest income, loans, deposits, and fee revenue, with full synergies from acquisitions expected by mid-2026.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock declined 6.02% due to missing revenue and EPS estimates, rising criticized asset ratio (3.79% to 4.23%), and 10% expected expense increase in 2026. However, positive guidance on revenue growth and acquisition synergies partially offset the negative reaction.
